identifiers: Placeholders stand in only for values, never for identifiers like column or table names; validate the chosen column against an allowlist of known-good names and reject anything else
why-parameterize: The query structure is compiled before the input is ever seen, and the value is then bound as pure data that is never parsed as SQL — so no input can become code
blocklist: Blocklists are trivially bypassed — UNunionION, different casing, and encodings all slip through — so they supplement but never replace parameterizing

layers.Least privilege: Caps the blast radius of a successful injection
layers.Safe error handling: Denies attackers a schema map from leaked error text
layers.Allowlist validation: The only safe way to handle identifiers like a sort column
